notes: Known as 'the Just', referenced as a figure of integrity in Roman-era historical context, likely Aristides the Just, an Athenian statesman famed for fairness.

docs.title: BOOK XXX
docs.author: Ammianus Marcellinus
docs.excerpt: … come from the school of Aristides the Just, or of Cato--men who, having bought public offices for large sums of money, proceed like troublesome creditors to hunt out every one's fortune, and so shake booty for themselves out of the laps of others. 22.
